index.title: Loot Dive Wiki | Complete Gear, Monster & Dungeon Database
index.description: Official wiki for the mobile game Loot Dive. A reference database covering all {{itemCount}} equipment pieces, {{monsterCount}} monsters, and {{dungeonCount}} dungeons. Useful for build crafting and efficient farming in this auto-battle hack-and-slash RPG.

index.heading: Loot Dive Wiki
index.intro: Master data reference for the auto-battle hack & slash RPG Loot Dive. Browse gear, monsters, and dungeons to craft builds and optimize your farming.

modDescriptions.hp_regen_to_atk_pct: {{value}}% of HP regen converts to ATK
modDescriptions.warlord_enrage: Warlord's Rage (Triggers once at HP ≤30%. Attack speed +20%, HP on hit +300)
modDescriptions.follow_up_attack_pct: Twin Blades (Every attack triggers a follow-up for {{value}}% of ATK)
modDescriptions.royal_roar: Royal Roar (Every 3 attacks: cleanse self of poison/ignite/chill)

uber_uber_goblin_king: Stack damage-defer and healing mods to survive the high damage output
Damage-reduction % from specific uniques or random armor mods is highly recommended
King's Slam can one-shot you. Freezing the Goblin King resets the attack counter
The stomp's Royal Roar on UberUber Goblin may become useful
uber_uber_bandit_leader: Stack damage-defer and healing mods to survive the fast attacks
Damage-reduction % from specific uniques or random armor mods is highly recommended
Since attack speed is high, chill-based slowdown is effective
A freeze chance lets you heal during the freeze window
The UberUber Twin Strike ring is a hyper-offensive item

system.title: Battle System Guide | Loot Dive Wiki
system.description: Loot Dive battle system guide. Base damage formula, critical hits, attack speed (ATB), follow-up attacks, ailments (poison/ignite/chill/freeze), defense mechanics, and awakening/keystone effects — all covered with formulas.
system.keywords: Loot Dive system, Loot Dive ailments, Loot Dive poison, Loot Dive ignite, Loot Dive chill, Loot Dive freeze, damage defer, damage reduction, critical, attack speed, keystone, Loot Dive wiki
system.heading: Battle System Guide
system.intro: Calculation rules that underpin gear choice and build crafting. Numbers reflect default settings (subject to balance changes).

damageFormula.heading: Base Damage Formula
damageFormula.intro: Normal-attack damage is derived from ATK and DEF with the following formula. Ailment and follow-up damage is computed based on this "base damage".
formulas: Total reduction = DEF reduction + damage-reduction MOD
Base damage = max(1, floor(ATK × (1 − total reduction)))
notes: "Damage reduction %" MODs are added to DEF reduction
Crits multiply base damage by ×3.0 + crit damage MODs

critical.heading: Critical
critical.intro: Crit% rolls on each normal attack. On success, damage is multiplied by a high factor. With a base multiplier of ×3.0, crit-focused builds scale exceptionally well.
formulas: Crit damage = base damage × crit multiplier
notes: Follow-up on crit (Twin Blades / Uber Twin Blades fixed mod): On crit, an extra ATK×0.5 hit (HP-on-hit re-triggers)
HP on crit (passive tree): adds flat HP regen on top of HP-on-hit, only when crit
Crit damage absorb% (passive tree): Converts a % of crit damage into HP
critical.footnote: Warriors start with crit +10%, making crit builds easy to enter. Hitting 100% crit rate stabilizes DPS and makes expected-value math simpler.

gauge.heading: Attack Speed / Gauge Battle (ATB)
gauge.intro: Players and enemies each fill a gauge; reaching 100 triggers one action. Attack speed (AS) directly drives gauge-fill rate — higher AS means more actions.
notes: Under Chill, the AS multiplier slows gauge fill
Under Freeze, gauge fill is completely halted
gauge.asModHeading: Two kinds of Attack Speed MODs
gauge.asModIntro: For players familiar with PoE: attack-speed MODs come as increased and more, each computed differently.
asModBox: increased (additive): All sum, then apply once. +30% & +20% → sum +50% → ×1.50
Order: Final AS = base AS × (1 + sum of increased / 100) × more multipliers ×...

followUp.heading: Follow-up Attacks
followUp.intro: Extra hits that trigger alongside the main attack. Stack them to land multiple hits per swing.
followUp.subIntro: Follow-up effects are almost always fixed mods on specific unique gear — you acquire them through equipment rather than random mods or the tree.
cards: Follow-up on crit (Twin Blades / Uber Twin Blades): On crit only, an ATK×0.5 follow-up. HP-on-hit re-triggers, so both DPS and sustain rise together.
UberUber Twin Blades' per-attack follow-up: Every attack triggers an ATK × X% follow-up. Crit-independent, so it fires even on low-crit builds.
King's Slam (UberUber Goblin Stomp): Every 5 attacks, ATK×3 super follow-up. Fires with the main attack, effectively ATK×4 damage.
Uber Crit Follow-up (Uber-tree keystone): Adds +1 hit to crit follow-up (ATK×1.0). A huge boost for crit-focused builds.

poison.heading: Poison
poison.intro: On normal attack, poison applies by "Poison chance%". Stackable — multiple instances can run (default cap 1, expandable via passives/MODs).
box: Stacks: At cap, new applications fail (wait out or consume)
poison.note: Poisoning an enemy triggers "Damage taken from poisoned enemies -% MOD", also softening incoming damage.

ignite.heading: Ignite
ignite.intro: Rolled via "Ignite chance%". On success, applies to the target. Overwriting — it doesn't stack; reapplication refreshes duration and damage.
box: Tick speed: Ignite tick speed+% MOD shortens intervals (denser damage, higher total)
Ignite damage: Ignite damage+% MOD raises per-tick power
ignite.note: Total damage is lower than poison (×6.0), but the overwrite model keeps stats fresh and higher attack speed still yields many applications.

chill.heading: Chill
chill.intro: Rolled via "Chill chance%". On success, reduces the enemy's attack speed. Overwriting.
box: ・ Chill effect 100% or more → ×0.5 (-50%, hard cap)
chill.note: A "reduce actions" ailment rather than damage. Especially effective against high-AS bosses (e.g. UberUber Bandit Leader).

freeze.heading: Freeze
freeze.intro: Rolled via "Freeze chance%". On success, fully halts the enemy. Action-counter skills (King's Slam etc.) also have their counters reset.
box: Chance cap: 10% hard cap (MODs never push it above 10%)
After expiry: Auto-transitions to chill from the same MOD (near-seamless slowdown)
freeze.note: One of the few ways to fully stun enemies by chance. The 10% cap holds, but high attack speed means more rolls and more openings.

defense.heading: Defense
defense.intro: Two categories: damage mitigation and sustain. Higher-difficulty bosses usually demand a mix.

damageReduction.heading: Damage Reduction %
damageReduction.intro: Cuts incoming damage by a percentage. Added to DEF reduction, so combines well with high-DEF gear.
damageReduction.note: Very strong mitigation, but hard to obtain. Currently only available through armor random mods (-5%) or select uniques. If endgame damage is an issue, start by hunting armor mods.

damageDefer.heading: Damage Defer %
damageDefer.intro: Delays part of incoming damage, spreading it over 4 seconds instead of instantly. Pairs well with HP-per-second sustain and helps avoid one-shots.
box: Effect: X% of incoming damage is received evenly over 4 seconds instead of instantly
damageDefer.note: If you out-heal the spread with HP/s regen, effective damage drops dramatically. Strong against single burst hits like King's Slam.

hpOnHit.heading: HP on Hit
hpOnHit.intro: You heal a flat amount every time you damage an enemy. Does not trigger on 0 damage.
box: With HP on crit MOD, crits give additional healing
Trigger: damage > 0 (normal and follow-up attacks; ailment damage is separate)
Re-trigger: Follow-up attacks also apply HP on Hit
hpOnHit.note: Combines powerfully with attack-speed and follow-up MODs — more hits, more healing. A foundational defensive layer.

hpRegen.heading: HP Regen per Second
hpRegen.intro: Automatically heals HP each second. Comes in flat and max-HP % flavors.
hpRegen.note: Combined with damage defer, a "heal-while-deferring" loop forms, turning burst damage into sustainable encounters.

poisonAbsorb.heading: Poison Lifesteal %
poisonAbsorb.intro: Converts a % of the poison damage you're dealing back into HP regen.
box: Effect: Heal (per-second poison damage across all stacks) × absorb%
Target: Applies to poison you deal — not to poison on yourself
poisonAbsorb.note: The sustain backbone of poison builds. Synergizes with poison-stack expansion passives/MODs.

igniteAbsorb.heading: Ignite Lifesteal %
igniteAbsorb.intro: Converts a % of ignite damage dealt back into HP regen.
box: Characteristic: Ignite ticks every 0.3s, so healing arrives in frequent small increments — stable in drawn-out fights
igniteAbsorb.note: Sustain backbone for ignite builds. Ignite tick-speed+% MODs shorten intervals and speed up healing.

counter.heading: Counter
counter.intro: A passive-tree effect: "Counter for a % of DEF when hit". Instantly reflects DEF × % damage back at the attacker.
box: Effect: Per hit taken, reflect DEF × counter% damage
Example: DEF 500, counter 20% → 100 damage to enemy per hit taken
counter.note: More reflects trigger against high-AS enemies — pairs well with high-enemy-AS / low-player-AS builds. Triggers on being hit rather than attacking, so no-hit builds won't benefit.

timeBuff.heading: Time-Based Buffs
timeBuff.intro: Effects that favor longer fights. One stack every 5 seconds, unlimited stacking. All appear as fixed MODs on Uber unique gear, so they require the relevant item to function.
cards: ATK +X% per 5s (Uber Goblin Blade / Uber End Blade): Every 5 seconds, +X to ATK increased%. +2X at 10s, +6X at 30s…
DEF +X% per 5s (Uber Goblin Armor / Uber End Armor): Same, for DEF increased%
HP regen +N per 5s (UberUber Goblin Stomp): Adds +N flat to HP per second regen
timeBuff.footnote: Designed to reward long boss fights. If you can absorb early damage (damage defer, HP/s regen, high HP), stacks build up and DPS explodes.

awakening.heading: Awakening / Keystones
awakening.intro: Special effects unlocked deep in the trees. Most are build-defining.
passiveCards: Normal Damage Negation: Normal-attack damage becomes 0, but you fully specialize into ailment / follow-up damage. Keystone for poison/ignite builds.
Slow-burning Flame: Every 5 ignite applications, ignite damage +10% (cap +200%). Boosts ignite long-fight damage.
Ignite Contagion: When an ignited enemy dies, ignite transfers to the next enemy. Dramatically speeds up clearing.
uberCards: Heavy Strike: AS -20% / 100% of damage as HP lifesteal / per attack, apply a "wound" stack (cap 5, each stack makes the enemy take ×1.2 damage, up to ×2.488).
Defensive Conversion: Adds DEF + maxHP/2 to ATK. Tank-builds-that-hit-hard fantasy.
Uber Crit Follow-up: Adds +1 hit to crit follow-up at ATK×1.0 (equal to a normal attack). Best-in-slot node for crit builds.
Venom Awakening: Effective poison stack multiplier +50% (×1.5). Pure DPS boost for poison builds.
Blazing Tempo: Ignite duration halved, tick interval halved (×2 damage density). Maximizes ignite burst for short fights.
Damage vs Chilled/Frozen: Damage dealt to chilled or frozen enemies multiplied. The finisher for cold builds.
awakening.footnote: Because these nodes define your build axis, tuning gear and random mods around your keystone maximizes the payoff.
